question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial of service by sending requests with value > 0 and a valid 'from' address but no 'to' address, given that hasTo() validation allows this when data contains valid init bytecode, triggering expensive contract deployment simulation in processCall() that consumes maximum EVM resources?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: constructServiceParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial of service by sending requests where the 'data' field is a valid hex string of maximum length (262,146 chars) with estimate=true, causing hexToBytes() to allocate a 131,072-byte array that is then passed to BinaryGasEstimator for up to 20 EVM iterations, each copying and processing the full callData payload?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronized gasLimitBucket to cause a thundering herd problem by sending bursts of 500 concurrent requests simultaneously, causing all threads to block on the synchronized bucket.tryConsume() call, creating a thread pile-up that exhausts the servlet container's thread pool and prevents new connections from being accepted?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the rateLimitBucket and gasLimitBucket are separate to bypass gas throttling by sending many requests with gas=21,000 (minimum), consuming only 2 scaled gas tokens per request (21000/10000=2) while still consuming 1 rate-limit token each, allowing the attacker to send 500 requests/second that collectively consume 1,000 gas tokens/second while staying under the rate limit?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial of service by exploiting the requestTimeout=10000ms configuration, sending requests that intentionally trigger slow EVM execution paths (e.g., complex loops in contract bytecode via the data field) that hold servlet threads for the full 10 seconds, exhausting the thread pool with only 50 concurrent requests?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication or API key requirements on POST /api/v1/contracts/call to launch a distributed denial of service attack from multiple IPs, each consuming up to 500 RPS from the shared rateLimitBucket, since the bucket is global and not partitioned per client?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial of service by sending requests with estimate=true and a 'to' address pointing to a contract with an infinite loop or extremely gas-intensive function, given that BinaryGasEstimator.search() will run up to 20 iterations each executing the full EVM call, and the safeCall() wrapper silently swallows exceptions allowing the loop to continue?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the token restoration logic in the catch block to perform a gas token inflation attack by crafting requests that always trigger InvalidParametersException after throttle() consumes tokens, causing throttleManager.restore(request.getGas()) to add tokens back, and if the restore adds more tokens than were consumed (due to scaling differences), gradually inflating the bucket beyond its capacity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial of service by exploiting the fact that ContractCallContext.run() creates a new context per request, and under high concurrency the ThreadLocal storage for ContractCallContext accumulates, causing memory pressure that triggers GC pauses and degrades throughput to the point where new transactions cannot be processed?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial of service by sending requests with a 'block' field set to a very large decimal block number that does not exist, causing recordFileService.findByBlockType() to perform an expensive database query that returns no result and throws BlockNumberNotFoundException, while still consuming gas tokens from the gasLimitBucket that are not restored since BlockNumberNotFoundException is not InvalidParametersException?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the gasLimitBucket capacity equals gasPerSecond (default 7,500,000,000 / 10,000 = 750,000 tokens) to calculate that sending requests with gas=15,000,000 (1,500 tokens each) requires only 500 requests to exhaust the bucket, which is achievable within 1 second given the 500 RPS rate limit?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial of service by sending requests with estimate=true and gas=15,000,000 to a contract address that always reverts, given that BinaryGasEstimator.safeCall() catches all exceptions and returns null (treating them as failures), causing the binary search to always take the maximum number of iterations (20) before converging, maximizing CPU consumption per request?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection-level rate limiting to open many persistent HTTP/1.1 keep-alive connections to the /api/v1/contracts/call endpoint, each sending requests at the maximum rate, multiplying the effective request rate beyond the intended 500 RPS limit and causing the service to become unresponsive?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial of service by exploiting the Stopwatch and logging in processCall() (log.debug with params, stopwatch, stringResult) to trigger excessive string formatting overhead under high load, given that ContractExecutionParameters.toString() may serialize large callData arrays, causing CPU saturation from string operations?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial of service by sending requests with value=Long.MAX_VALUE and a valid 'from' address, triggering expensive balance validation logic in the EVM execution layer that checks whether the sender has sufficient balance, consuming disproportionate CPU time per request?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the 'from' field is optional (nullable) to send requests without a 'from' address, causing Address.ZERO to be used as the sender, which may bypass certain EVM pre-checks and allow execution paths that consume more resources than authenticated calls?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial of service by exploiting the fact that isStaticCall is hardcoded to false in constructServiceParameters(), allowing all simulated calls to attempt state modifications in the EVM, which may trigger more expensive execution paths (e.g., SSTORE operations) that consume more CPU and memory than read-only calls?
